skill.name: senior-data-scientist
skill.description: Data science assistant for experiment design, statistical analysis, feature engineering, model evaluation, MLOps planning, and stakeholder communication. It provides reference guidance and lightweight Python script templates for data science workflows.
skill.summary: Plan experiments, engineer features, evaluate models, and communicate data science decisions.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static weak-cryptography and network-reconnaissance findings to be false positives caused by data science and systems terminology. The skill includes documented shell commands and placeholder Python scripts, but no evidence of malicious execution, exfiltration, network access, or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: Documented Shell Commands Require User Review
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains example commands for Python scripts, tests, formatting, Docker, kubectl, and Helm. These are documentation examples rather than self-executing code, but users should review commands before running them in a real environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly visible in fenced bash blocks and are not invoked automatically by the skill. The risk is low because execution requires an explicit user action.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography locations contain prose such as production design, performance, caching, encryption, and reliability guidance. No cryptographic algorithm implementation or insecure hash usage was found at the reviewed locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed flagged files show documentation and simple argparse-based placeholders, not weak cryptographic primitives. The repeated high severity detections are explained by keyword collisions rather than executable crypto code.

low_findings.title: Static Network-Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged reference lines describe resource awareness and performance planning. No socket use, port scanning, network probing, or remote host enumeration was found in the reviewed reference files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed text is generic performance guidance and contains no network APIs or host targets. This supports a high-confidence false-positive classification.

content.value_statement: Data science teams need clear methods for experiments, features, models, and decisions. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured analysis and production-aware planning.
actual_capabilities: Guides experiment design, A/B testing plans, metrics, and decision criteria.
Supports feature engineering planning with validation, monitoring, and scalability considerations.
Provides model evaluation guidance for metrics, drift, deployment readiness, and stakeholder reporting.
Includes lightweight Python script templates for experiment, feature, and evaluation workflows.
Summarizes production data science practices across testing, monitoring, privacy, and compliance.
limitations: The included Python scripts are placeholders and do not implement full data processing logic.
It does not validate statistical assumptions unless the user provides data context and methodology details.
It does not connect to databases, cloud services, or model registries by itself.
Recommendations require human review before use in regulated or high-stakes decisions.

use_cases.title: Plan Product Experiments
use_cases.description: Define hypotheses, metrics, sample size considerations, guardrails, and decision rules for A/B tests.

use_cases.title: Prepare Model Evaluation Reviews
use_cases.description: Assess model performance, failure modes, drift risks, deployment readiness, and business tradeoffs.

use_cases.title: Design Analytics Roadmaps
use_cases.description: Translate business goals into data sources, analytical methods, milestones, risks, and stakeholder outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Define an Experiment
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design an A/B test for [product change]. Include hypothesis, primary metric, guardrail metrics, risks, and decision criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Review Feature Ideas
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these candidate features for [model goal]. Identify leakage risks, validation checks, monitoring needs, and production concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate a Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this model evaluation summary for [use case]. Explain metric tradeoffs, segment performance, drift risks, and deployment readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Data Science Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a data science roadmap for [business objective]. Include methods, data requirements, experiment plan, model lifecycle, governance, and stakeholder communication.

output_examples.input: Design an experiment to test a new onboarding flow.
output: A hypothesis tied to activation rate and user completion behavior.
Primary and guardrail metrics with data quality checks.
A decision framework covering rollout, hold, or redesign outcomes.

output_examples.input: Review a churn prediction model before deployment.
output: A model readiness summary with metric tradeoffs and segment checks.
A risk list covering leakage, drift, calibration, and operational ownership.
A stakeholder explanation focused on business impact and next actions.

best_practices: Provide business goals, available data, constraints, and decision timelines before asking for recommendations.
Ask for assumptions, risks, and validation checks when designing experiments or models.
Review generated plans with domain experts before making production or policy decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not treat placeholder scripts as complete production pipelines.
Do not run documented shell commands without checking paths, permissions, and environment impact.
Do not use model recommendations without validating data quality and statistical assumptions.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan experiments, feature engineering, model evaluation, causal analysis, and production data science workflows.

faq.question: Does it process real datasets automatically?
faq.answer: No. The scripts are lightweight templates, and users must provide or implement real processing logic.

faq.question: Can it help with A/B testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure hypotheses, metrics, guardrails, risks, and decision criteria for controlled experiments.

faq.question: Can it support MLOps planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers monitoring, drift checks, deployment readiness, retraining, and production ownership.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found no malicious behavior. Users should still review documented shell commands before running them.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the supported tools metadata.
